mnemonics.title: CRITICAL SECTION REQUIREMENTS
mnemonics.content: MUTUAL EXCLUSION: ONLY ONE IN CRITICAL SECTION AT A TIME. PROGRESS: IF NOBODY IN AND SOME WANT IN, PICK ONE. BOUNDED WAITING: LIMIT ON HOW MANY CAN ENTER AFTER I REQUEST.

mnemonics.title: SEMAPHORE OPERATIONS
mnemonics.content: WAIT: DECREMENT, IF NEGATIVE THEN BLOCK. SIGNAL: INCREMENT, IF THERE ARE WAITERS, WAKE ONE UP. REMEMBER: P-V OPERATIONS (PROBEREN-VERHOOGEN IN DUTCH).

mnemonics.title: PRODUCER-CONSUMER BUFFER
mnemonics.content: BUFFER FULL WHEN (IN+1) % N = OUT. BUFFER EMPTY WHEN IN = OUT. NUMBER OF ITEMS = (N - OUT + IN) % N.

mnemonics.title: READERS-WRITERS RULES
mnemonics.content: READERS CAN READ TOGETHER. WRITERS NEED EXCLUSIVE ACCESS. READERS INCREASE READCOUNT. WRITER WAITS IF READCOUNT > 0 OR WRITING = TRUE.

acronyms.expansion: Compare-And-Swap - atomic instruction for lock-free synchronization
acronyms.usage: Used in multithreaded programs to update shared variables without locks

acronyms.expansion: Proberen (test/wait) and Verhogen (signal/increment) - Dutch terms for semaphore operations
acronyms.usage: Alternate names for wait() and signal() operations on semaphores
Used to implement spinlocks in multiprocessor systems
One way to ensure bounded waiting in critical section solutions

visualTips.visualization: DRAW TWO ROWS FOR PROCESSES P0 AND P1. USE TWO VARIABLES: 'TURN' (SHOW WHO'S TURN) AND 'FLAG[2]' (SHOW WHO WANTS TO ENTER). ARROWS SHOW FLOW BETWEEN 'WHILE CONDITIONS' AND 'CRITICAL SECTION'.
DRAW A NUMBER LINE WITH SEMAPHORE VALUE. NEGATIVE VALUES SHOW HOW MANY PROCESSES ARE WAITING. WHEN SIGNAL CALLED, MOVE FROM WAIT QUEUE TO READY QUEUE.

visualTips.visualize: DRAW A CIRCLE WITH N SLOTS. 'IN' POINTER MOVES CLOCKWISE ON PRODUCER. 'OUT' POINTER MOVES CLOCKWISE ON CONSUMER. BOTH WRAP AROUND USING MODULO.
DRAW FIVE PHILOSOPHERS IN A CIRCLE WITH A FORK BETWEEN EACH PAIR. THINK STATE = NO FORKS. HUNGRY STATE = WAITING FOR FORKS. EATING STATE = HAS TWO FORKS.

analogies.analogy: THINK OF A SINGLE BATHROOM IN A HOUSE. ONLY ONE PERSON CAN USE IT AT A TIME. THE BATHROOM IS THE CRITICAL SECTION.
A PARKING LOT WITH N SPOTS HAS A COUNTER SHOWING AVAILABLE SPOTS. WHEN A CAR ENTERS, COUNTER DECREMENTS. WHEN LEAVES, INCREMENTS. IF ZERO, CARS WAIT.
A KEY TO A ROOM. ONLY ONE PERSON CAN HAVE THE KEY AT A TIME. IF SOMEONE HAS IT, OTHERS WAIT AT THE DOOR.
A LIBRARY. MANY PEOPLE CAN READ BOOKS SIMULTANEOUSLY (SILENTLY). BUT TO WRITE OR EDIT A BOOK, YOU NEED THE WHOLE LIBRARY TO YOURSELF.
WAITING AT A DOOR KNOB, KEEP TRYING THE HANDLE EVERY FEW SECONDS UNTIL IT OPENS. WASTES ENERGY BUT WORKS FOR SHORT WAITS.
A BUFFET RESTAURANT. CHEFS (PRODUCERS) PUT FOOD ON THE TABLE. GUESTS (CONSUMERS) TAKE FOOD. TABLE HAS LIMITED SPACE - CHEFS WAIT IF FULL, GUESTS WAIT IF EMPTY.
